title: Byrsella bonplandii
taxonomy: Byrsella bonplandii is classified within the family Byrsellaceae. It belongs to the order Ustilaginales, which is a group of smut fungi. Byrsella bonplandii is a type of fungus that infects plants, specifically members of the family Piperaceae. The exact classification of Byrsella bonplandii is further detailed in the kingdom Fungi. More specifically, it is classified as a genus within the phylum Basidiomycota.

intro: Byrsella bonplandii is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Sapindaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds, as well as alleviate symptoms of respiratory tract infections.
